title: Fix puzzle bug: async checkMove race condition

source.notes: Upstream commit from Mar 2026. Fixes race condition in puzzle auto-advance.

description: When 'jump to next puzzle immediately' is enabled and the user solves a puzzle, the completion callback and puzzle generation fire synchronously. This causes a race condition where the old puzzle state hasn't reset before the new puzzle loads. The fix makes checkMove async and awaits both changeCompletion and generatePuzzle, then calls reset() before returning.
notes: Key changes: (1) checkMove becomes async, (2) changeCompletion and generatePuzzle signatures become Promise-returning, (3) await both + reset() after generatePuzzle
The Puzzles.tsx change updates the prop type signatures from sync to async

in_scope: Add reset() call after generatePuzzle in the jump-to-next path
Update function signatures in Puzzles.tsx to return Promise<void>

requirements: Solving a puzzle with 'jump to next immediately' advances to next puzzle cleanly
Puzzle board resets properly between puzzles (no stale pieces)
Incorrect move still marks puzzle as incorrect
edge_cases: Rapid-fire solving — solve 3 puzzles quickly in sequence
Solving the last puzzle in the database
Solving with 'jump to next' disabled (should still work as before)

acceptance_gates: Puzzle solving with auto-advance works without visual glitches
Puzzle solving without auto-advance still works

manual: Open Puzzles, enable 'jump to next puzzle immediately' in settings
Solve 5 puzzles in a row — board should reset cleanly between each
Disable 'jump to next' and solve a puzzle — should show completion, not auto-advance
Intentionally fail a puzzle, then solve the next — completion should say 'incorrect' for the first

done_criteria: checkMove is async with awaited callbacks
reset() called after generatePuzzle in auto-advance path
Manual puzzle testing confirms no visual glitches
